report_name: HHS Prepares to Launch Mandatory Minimum Cybersecurity Standards for U.S. Hospitals

identities.name: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS)
identities.description: The U.S. federal agency responsible for creating and enforcing new mandatory minimum cybersecurity standards for hospitals, based on the Cybersecurity Performance Goals (CPGs).

identities.name: U.S. hospitals
identities.description: The group of healthcare organizations in the United States that will be subject to new mandatory minimum cybersecurity standards from HHS.

identities.name: Medicare/Medicaid
identities.description: U.S. government healthcare programs whose payment and reimbursement systems are proposed to be used as an enforcement mechanism for the new cybersecurity regulations, with potential penalties for non-compliant hospitals starting in fiscal year 2029.

relationships.source_name: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS)
